extraction.one_line_summary: BERNAL Egan secured Paris-Nice by limiting losses after QUINTANA Nairo's sub-50km attack, while IZAGIRRE Ion took the stage from the breakaway.
tactical_patterns: Quintana attacked under 50km out with teammates ahead acting as landing pads, claiming intermediate sprint bonuses targeting virtual lead.
Sky allowed 40-rider break with non-GC threats, controlled tempo only when Quintana made virtual lead tangible near 30km mark.
Izagirre countered from leading group on descent into Nice, capitalizing on Van Garderen's spent effort and peloton's Quintana focus.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: only clawed back 7 seconds despite coordinated attack with teammates positioned ahead, insufficient to threaten 45-second deficit
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: at 22 showed maturity managing pressure rather than overreacting to Quintana's late-race attack with teammates
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: strong enough for third overall but riding in service of younger teammate Bernal
rider_signals.IZAGIRRE Ion: positive: countered Van Garderen at optimal moment when peloton fixated on GC battle, reading race dynamics perfectly
